name: investor-pitch
description: Prepare for fundraising conversations. Research the market, tear down competitors, model your pricing, calculate runway, and produce a pitch-ready checklist. Walk in knowing your numbers cold.

config.instruction: Research the market landscape for the business described. Identify market size (TAM/SAM/SOM), growth rate, key trends, regulatory environment, and timing factors. Output structured market data that an investor would want to see.

Identify and analyze the top 5-8 competitors. For each: what they do, pricing, funding raised, strengths, weaknesses, and how the user's product differentiates. Produce a competitive positioning matrix.

Model 3 pricing scenarios (conservative, moderate, aggressive) based on the market research. For each: monthly revenue at 100/1K/10K customers, gross margin, unit economics (CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC ratio). Recommend the strongest pricing strategy.

Calculate runway under each pricing scenario. Inputs: current cash, monthly burn, revenue ramp assumptions. Output: months of runway, break-even point, how much to raise, and what milestones that buys.

Build the investor pitch checklist: problem statement, solution, market size, traction, business model, competitive moat, team, financials, ask amount, use of funds. Flag any weak spots that need more work before pitching.
